zh.South Denchworth, Denchworth Manor, East: 英國英格蘭波克夏丹華斯
zh.South Denchworth, Berkshire, England: 英國英格蘭波克夏丹華斯
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth於大約1436年出生在英國藍卡爾巴頓。她於1454年與John Hyde II在英國英格蘭達勒姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她於1598年住在英國英格蘭爾瑪波且於1598年住在英國爾瑪波。她去世於1478年5月29日在英國波克夏丹華斯享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth于大约1436年出生在英国藍卡爾巴頓。她于1454年与John Hyde II在英国英格兰達勒姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她于1598年住在英国英格兰爾瑪波且于1598年住在英国爾瑪波。她去世于1478年5月29日在英国波克夏丹華斯享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth was born about 1436, in Barton, Lancashire, England. She married John Hyde II in 1454, in Durham, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She lived in Marple, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom in 1598 and Marple, Cheshire, England in 1598. She died on 29 May 1478, in Denchworth, Berkshire, England, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth est née environ 1436, à Barton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John Hyde II en 1454, à Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle a vécu à Marple, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1598 et à Marple, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1598. Elle est décédée le 29 mai 1478, à Denchworth, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth wurde um 1436 in Barton, Lancashire, England geboren. Sie hat 1454 in Durham, England, United Kingdom John Hyde II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1598 hat sie in Marple, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und 1598 in Marple, Cheshire, England gelebt. Sie starb am 29. Mai 1478 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Denchworth, Berkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth è nata il circa 1436 nel Barton, Lancashire, England. Lei sposato John Hyde II nel 1454, nel Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Vissuto a Marple, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1598 e nel Marple, Cheshire, England nel 1598. Morì il 29 maggio 1478, nel Denchworth, Berkshire, England, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Boothは約1436年にイギリスランカシュアイアバートンで生まれた。1454年にイギリスイギリスダルハムでJohn Hyde IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1598年にはイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアマルプルに，1598年にはイギリスチェシュアイアマルプルに住んでいた。彼女は1478年5月29日にイギリスバークシュアイアデンチウオルスで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth(은)는 대략 1436년 영국 랭카실르 바턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1454년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람에서 John Hyde II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1598년도에 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 마플에서, 1598년도에 영국 체실르 마플에서 살았다. 그녀는 1478년 5월 29일 영국 버크실르 덴ch워스에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth nasceu aproximadamente 1436, em Barton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John Hyde II em 1454, em Durham, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela viveu em Marple, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1598 e em Marple, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1598. Ela faleceu em 29 de maio de 1478, em Denchworth, Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth родилась около 1436 г. в местности бартон, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hyde II в 1454 г. в местности дурам, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она жила в местности марпл, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в 1598 году и в местности марпл, чешир, Англия в 1598 году. Она умерла 29 мая 1478 г. в местности денчворт, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Booth nació aproximadamente 1436, en Barton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Se casó con John Hyde II en 1454, en Durham, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Vivió en Marple, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1598 y en Marple, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1598. Murió el 29 de mayo de 1478, en Denchworth, Berkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años.
